methodsAttempted.query: site:dlsu.edu.ph De La Salle University artificial intelligence policy generative AI students faculty academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned an official DLSU-hosted PDF, Policies on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education, and several Animo Repository research outputs.
A follow-up official-domain search primarily returned repository research papers and a policy brief record, so direct claims were limited to the official PDF policy.
The official PDF host returned robots.txt with User-agent: * and an empty Disallow directive; the PDF returned HTTP 200 with content-type application/pdf.

methodsAttempted.query: DLSU policy for generative AI in education PDF
methodsAttempted.notes: The PDF was fetched by HTTP and parsed with pdftotext; Firecrawl PDF parsing also returned the same substantive policy sections.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one strong DLSU source: the Office of the Provost-hosted Policies on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education PDF. Animo Repository research outputs were recorded and rejected as primary policy evidence because they are scholarly or policy-brief materials rather than direct institutional rules.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policies on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education
artifacts.queryUsed: site:dlsu.edu.ph De La Salle University artificial intelligence policy generative AI students faculty academic integrity
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official DLSU-hosted PDF authored under the Office of the Provost path; the document title, purpose, scope, policy details, syllabus requirements, academic dishonesty provisions, AI detector guidance, and data privacy section are all visible in the fetched text.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Integrity in the Age of AI: Ethical Considerations of ChatGPT Use
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official repository-hosted research/conference content about ChatGPT ethics, not a direct DLSU institutional policy source for tracker claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Regulating Generative AI in Scholarly Works: A Policy Brief for Academic Publishers
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official repository record for a policy brief about academic publishing; rejected as primary evidence because it is not a DLSU-wide teaching, learning, or research rule.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: Search result described a study of the ethical implications and impacts of ChatGPT use among senior high school students at De La Salle University Manila.
artifacts.notes: Useful context for scholarship, but not used for claims because it is not an official institutional policy or guidance page.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The repository result describes a policy brief for academic publishers about regulating generative AI in scholarly works.
artifacts.notes: The item is relevant scholarship but not a direct DLSU policy for students or faculty; direct claims use the Office of the Provost PDF.

targets.sourceTitle: Policies on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education

stopConditions: Stop after the official generative AI policy PDF is fetched and parsed successfully.
Do not use repository scholarship or policy-brief records as direct institutional policy evidence.
Do not follow login-only, CAPTCHA, or unrelated external links.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policies on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: For students, the following shall be considered as generative AI-related academic dishonesty: Using generative AI in producing a submission in a way that is prohibited by the usage policy defined for that assessment, even if it was disclosed; Failing to properly disclose the use of generative AI in a submission.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The policy classifies prohibited generative AI use and failure to disclose generative AI use as academic dishonesty.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section IV.d, Handling of Generative AI-Related Academic Dishonesty

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policies on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faculty can use AI detector tools (Turnitin, GPTZero, etc.) for flagging suspicious submissions. However, results of such tools cannot be used as the sole basis for establishing generative AI-related academic dishonesty, as these tools can produce both false positives and false negatives.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI detector results may flag submissions but cannot be the sole basis for establishing generative AI-related academic dishonesty.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status claim because the source explicitly states scope and is an official DLSU policy PDF.
Approved because the quoted disclosure requirement applies to both faculty and students and uses mandatory language.
Approved because the source explicitly says every course must include the dedicated syllabus section starting Term 1, 2025-2026.
Approved with two evidence records: one for the dishonesty definition and one for the AI detector limitation.
Approved because the source directly prohibits sharing personal, sensitive, internal, proprietary, and confidential information with generative AI services.

artifacts.title: De La Salle University Generative AI Policy Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found an official De La Salle University PDF policy for generative AI in higher education. Candidate claims cover policy scope, written disclosure, syllabus AI-use declarations, academic dishonesty treatment including AI detector limits, and privacy/confidentiality boundaries. Animo Repository research outputs were rejected as primary evidence because they are not direct institutional policy sources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The run used one verified official PDF as the source of policy claims; repository scholarship and policy-brief materials were not used as binding institutional policy evidence.
No login-gated help desk announcement referenced inside the PDF was accessed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
